response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack developer with breadth across TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and some C/C++ IDE work at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI), but the core requirements of this role — deep JVM expertise, strong Kotlin, and Spring ecosystem experience — are essentially absent from the resume. Java appears only as a secondary tool in older roles (robotic arms, mobile orchestration) and Kotlin/Spring are not mentioned at all. While the prior C IDE component work and language-tooling adjacency (D test runner, MCP code generators) show some relevant instincts, the gap from the required JVM/Spring stack is too large for a mid-senior IC hire focused on enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE features.
strengths: Prior experience building components for a commercial IDE (LabWindows/CVI) in C/C++
Strong software-craftsmanship background: TDD, testing tooling, large-scale codebases
Has shipped language-tooling and developer-tools work (D test runner, MCP generators)
concerns: No demonstrated Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience
No Kotlin experience and only limited, dated Java exposure
Career center of gravity is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not JVM internals

key_factors.factor: No Kotlin and weak Java track record
